title: dactylis glomerata subsp. hackelii
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. hackelii, also known as Hackel's orchard grass, is a subspecies of Dactylis glomerata, a type of grass native to Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Dactylis glomerata subsp. hackelii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
